trivia_bullets.text: Valorous Steed is an original card first printed in Core Set 2021 (released July 3, 2020) — not a reprint. Core Set 2021 was the last standalone core set Wizards of the Coast produced in the modern era, released alongside the Teferi-focused story and designed to be a teaching/entry-point set. [Scryfall]

Artist Donato Giancola has painted Valorous Steed across all three of its printings: Core Set 2021 (2020), Jumpstart 2022 (2022), and The List (2024). Giancola is a representational oil-on-panel painter known for medieval and Renaissance-influenced fantasy art; his Jumpstart unicorn Plains oil painting (a separate card) sold at auction for $10,100 in June 2020, within weeks of Valorous Steed's own release. [Scryfall prints search; Hipsters of the Coast, July 2020]

Mechanically, Valorous Steed creates a 2/2 Knight token with vigilance on entry — neatly reinforcing the flavor that the unicorn 'chooses' its knight companion. EDHREC rank 19,008 confirms it is a fringe card outside dedicated tribal lists. Legal in Pauper Commander (paupercommander) and Pioneer. [Scryfall; EDHREC rank from Scryfall API]

"A unicorn chooses only the most virtuous and noble of knights to be its companion." — no attributed speaker. The flavor text mirrors the card's enter-the-battlefield ability: the unicorn arrives and a knight token appears, enacting the lore beat mechanically. [Scryfall]

Community sentiment on Draftsim's unicorn ranking article: Valorous Steed is noted as a reasonable Limited role-player in Core Set 2021 draft but considered overcosted for Constructed — 5 mana for a 3/3 with a 2/2 token is functional but rarely exciting outside unicorn tribal lists. [Draftsim, 'All 32 Unicorns in Magic Ranked', via WebSearch snippet]
sources: Draftsim unicorn ranking article, via WebSearch snippet
